name: GenRank Public API
description: Read-only REST API exposing how strongly AI models recommend entities, organized by category. Questions are curated per category and polled monthly.
important: "www.genrank.com" is canonical. The apex "genrank.com" redirects (307, path preserved) to "www", so it also works for clients that follow redirects — prefer "www" to skip the extra hop.

authentication.note: All read endpoints are public — no API key required.

cors.note: Open CORS so browser clients (badges, widgets, dashboards) can call directly.

data_freshness.note: AI models are polled once per month (1st, UTC). Use last_polled_at in /api/rankings to see the latest poll.

concepts.category: A hierarchical bucket (e.g., Software > Developer Tools) holding a curated set of ranking questions.
concepts.entity: Anything that can be ranked — a company, product, person, place, etc.
concepts.genrank_score: A weighted visibility score. Higher rank position in AI answers and higher model market share => higher score.

endpoints.description: Category tree. Start here to discover category ids/slugs to query rankings with.

parameters.name: locale
parameters.description: Translate category names (e.g., ko). Falls back to default name.

parameters.name: tree
parameters.description: true => nested tree, false => flat list

response.categories: [{ id, slug, name, level, parent_id, display_order, children? }]

endpoints.description: Questions curated inside a category. Useful for drilling from a category into the individual ranked questions feeding it.
Entities ranked by GenRank score for a category (aggregated across its sub-categories).

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Category UUID or slug. Omit for all categories.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Filter entities by language (e.g., en, ko)

response.rankings: [{ entity_id, entity_name, entity_type, genrank_score, rank, change }]
response.pagination: { page, limit, totalCount, totalPages, hasNextPage, hasPreviousPage }

endpoints.description: Weekly leaderboard history for a single question — for per-question trend charts. Returns each entity that was top N in any week with rank/score per week.

response.entities: [{ entityId, entityName, history: [{ weekId, weekDate, rank, genrankScore }], currentRank, currentScore, rankChange }]

endpoints.description: Look up entities (and published questions) by name substring. Start here when you only have a brand name and need its entity id.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Restrict question results to one language

endpoints.description: Entity details and its GenRank score per published question.
Monthly ranking history for an entity (trend / movers analysis).

response.history: [{ pollRunId, pollDate, rank, genrankScore, weightedScore, rawScore, questionId }]

endpoints.description: Question detail with the latest raw AI responses and the entities each model extracted (with per-model scores).

response.question: { id, text, slug, language, ... }
response.responses: [{ id, response_text, polled_at, model_name, entities: [{ id, name, type, rank, score }] }]

endpoints.description: Related questions discovered via shared category and shared top entities.

response.related: [{ id, text, slug, vote_score, comment_count, created_at, language }]

endpoints.description: This document, as JSON. Also available at /api/agent/docs.

typical_workflow: GET /api/search?q=<brand> — find your entity id by name
GET /api/entities/{id} — score per question for that entity
